overview: This White/Blue control deck stabilizes games through efficient removal and recursive value generation. It leverages Lurrus of the Dream-Den to repeatedly cast Swords to Plowshares and replay resilient threats like Thing in the Ice. The strategy focuses on outlasting opponents by maintaining board control and converting card advantage into lethal damage.
win_conditions: Outlasting opponents through superior card advantage and recursion
Closing games with high-toughness, unblockable creatures
Generating infinite value loops via Lurrus and graveyard recursion

keep_criteria: Hands containing early disruption and a land drop
Hands with Lurrus or Swords to Plowshares alongside two lands
Hands featuring Thing in the Ice with a way to protect or trade it
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, at least one Swords to Plowshares or Dovin's Veto, and a turn-2 play.
Prioritize hands that can stabilize by turn 3.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking interaction against early aggressive boards
Hands with three or more lands and no removal
Hands without a clear path to stabilize or generate value
Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, 3+ lands with no plays, or hands lacking early interaction against aggro/combo.

early_game: Play lands and hold up white mana for Swords to Plowshares
Neutralize early threats to prevent being overwhelmed
Prioritize drawing into Lurrus to establish your recursion engine
Turn 2: Play second land, cast a 2-mana interaction (Dovin's Veto, Path to Exile, or Fog) or develop mana.
If facing aggro, prioritize Swords to Plowshares or Fog on turn 2 if possible.
Sequence lands to ensure colored mana availability for WU spells.
mid_game: Establish board presence with Thing in the Ice when safe
Use Lurrus to replay Swords for repeated removal and board clears
Maintain card advantage by trading efficiently and protecting key assets
Preserve mana for interaction until turn 4-5.
Don't overcommit to board presence early.
Use Lurrus to replay cheap permanents from the graveyard to maintain hand size and board control.
Track graveyard contents to avoid dead draws.
Hold back on casting Thing in the Ice until you have at least one removal spell or counter in hand, or until you can protect it.
Prioritize trading cheap permanents for opponent's threats.
late_game: Leverage Lurrus recursion to outvalue opponent's resource base
Stabilize with resilient creatures and sweepers
Close out games with unblockable attackers or repeated damage triggers
Thing in the Ice is the primary win condition.
Use Lurrus to recur Swords/Path to clear blockers or replay Fog to reset the board.
Card advantage from Thing's death trigger or Lurrus recursion closes games.
Go all-in when you have card advantage locked, opponent has no removal/counter backup, or you can safely cast Thing and follow up with protection/recursion.

key_cards.name: Thing in the Ice
key_cards.role: Primary resilient threat and win condition
key_cards.notes: Play it when you have removal or protection ready to prevent it from being killed by its own triggered ability, then use it to clear opposing boards and attack for lethal.

key_cards.name: Lurrus of the Dream-Den
key_cards.notes: Use it to replay Swords to Plowshares for repeated removal, or to bring back Thing in the Ice and other key creatures from the graveyard to maintain board presence.

key_cards.name: Swords to Plowshares
key_cards.notes: Target opponent's threats to exile them, or use it on your own creatures with Lurrus to generate repeated value and clear the board efficiently.

sequencing_tips: Hold up white mana for Swords to Plowshares before committing to attacks
Trigger Thing in the Ice's ability only when you have a way to save it or trade favorably
Use Lurrus to replay Swords before replaying creatures to maximize board control
Thing in the Ice dies to Swords to Plowshares, so don't cast it into your own removal.
Lurrus only recurs 2-mana or less permanents, so plan your graveyard accordingly.
Watch out for Jewel Shops artifact hate or Oath of Druids combo turns.
Avoid overcommitting to the board before turn 4.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early Swords to Plowshares on high-impact creatures and use Lurrus to replay removal efficiently.
Disrupt their casting sequence early and use Swords to exile key combo pieces before they resolve.
Race them with Thing in the Ice and leverage Lurrus to outvalue their resource base.

Use Swords to Plowshares or Path to Exile on their counterspells if they're creatures, or play around them by holding up interaction. Use Lurrus to recur cheap spells to pressure through. Consider Thing in the Ice as a threat that forces them to use multiple answers.
Prioritize early removal (Swords, Path) on ramp targets. Use Fog or Dovin's Veto to stall. Don't let them get ahead; trade efficiently.
Stabilize with Swords to Plowshares, Path to Exile, and Fog. Mulligan aggressively for early interaction. Use Lurrus to replay removal to handle multiple threats.
Value Lurrus recursion and Thing in the Ice triggers. Prioritize keeping interaction in hand. Win by out-grinding with graveyard recursion and forcing them to overextend.

common_threats: Combo pieces that win on cast
Board wipes that reset the board
Watch for graveyard accumulation (Dredge), artifact acceleration (Jewel Shops), and Oath targets.
Note when opponents tap out for big threats or combo pieces.
Track if they've answered your Lurrus or Thing.
